_comment: Tier-2 Layer-F: validates the catalog AS SHIPPED — the LLM-emit-and-run loop. Per backend, calls live-registry generate_input(backend, physics='poisson', variant='2d'), writes the emitted template to a tempfile, executes it through the repo .venv python, asserts exit_code==0 and no Traceback. This is the missing piece between Layer-B (generator doesn't raise) and Layer-C (re-implemented API converges): the actual template the catalog ships actually runs. Currently covers skfem + kratos (both run quickly in the repo .venv); fenics + ngsolve need their conda envs and would 5-10x the fixture runtime — left for separate iteration. Verified on 2026-06-01: both rc=0, no Traceback. Catches the regression class where a catalog template is well-formed Python (Layer B + Layer E pass) but raises at runtime due to a missing import, wrong PETSc option key, sign error in a manufactured-test source, etc.
